description.high: A task management web application with a REST API, persistent storage, and email notifications for due tasks — deployed as AWS Lambda functions.
description.tech: AWS Lambda handlers routed via API Gateway proxy events; repository pattern over DynamoDB via boto3; EventBridge Scheduler triggers overdue-task notifications via Amazon SES.

description.high: The filing cabinet. Saves tasks to DynamoDB so they survive Lambda cold starts, and finds them again on request.
description.tech: Repository pattern over AWS DynamoDB via boto3. Maps Task dataclasses to DynamoDB items; UUID-based partition keys; optional FilterExpression scans for status filtering. Table is provisioned externally (Terraform).

blocks.name: TaskRepository

description.high: Saves tasks to DynamoDB and retrieves them, with optional filtering by status.
description.tech: CRUD over a DynamoDB table. __init__ accepts a table_name string (default from DYNAMODB_TABLE env var, fallback 'tasks') and creates a boto3.resource('dynamodb').Table reference. No _migrate() — table is provisioned by Terraform. save(task) calls table.put_item, assigning a uuid4().hex id if task.id is None and storing due_date as ISO string. find(status) calls table.scan with an optional FilterExpression=Attr('status').eq(status) from boto3.dynamodb.conditions, then hydrates each item dict via Task.from_row.

inputs.name: table_name
inputs.description: DynamoDB table name; defaults to DYNAMODB_TABLE env var or 'tasks'

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: Task dataclass instance passed to save()

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Optional status filter string passed to find(); None returns all tasks

outputs.name: saved_task
outputs.description: Task returned by save() with id assigned from uuid4().hex

outputs.name: tasks
outputs.description: List of hydrated Task dataclasses returned by find()

blocks.name: Task

description.high: The shape of a single task: its id, title, due date, priority level, and whether it's done.
description.tech: Dataclass with fields id (str|None), title (str), due_date (datetime), priority (int, default 2), status (str, default 'pending'). Provides a from_row(row) classmethod that hydrates an instance from either a plain dict or any mapping (e.g. sqlite3.Row) using datetime.fromisoformat for due_date. The id field is str (UUID hex) to match DynamoDB usage.

inputs.name: row
inputs.description: Plain dict or mapping (e.g. DynamoDB item) passed to from_row(); must contain id, title, due_date, priority, status keys

outputs.name: task
outputs.description: Hydrated Task instance returned by from_row()

description.high: Sends you an email listing every overdue task so nothing slips through the cracks — delivered via Amazon SES.
description.tech: EmailNotifier reads AWS_SES_REGION and SES_FROM_EMAIL from environment variables. send_overdue_digest(overdue, to) renders a plaintext digest of overdue Task objects and delivers it via boto3 SES send_email. Returns 0 if the list is empty, 1 after a successful send.

blocks.name: EmailNotifier

description.high: Composes and sends a single digest email via Amazon SES listing every overdue task. Does nothing if there are no overdue tasks.
description.tech: Reads AWS_SES_REGION (default 'us-east-1') and SES_FROM_EMAIL from os.environ in __init__. Creates self.ses = boto3.client('ses', region_name=self.region). send_overdue_digest(overdue: list[Task], to: str) short-circuits and returns 0 when overdue is empty; otherwise builds a plaintext body from task.title and task.due_date, then calls self.ses.send_email with Source=self.from_email, Destination={'ToAddresses': [to]}, and a Message dict containing Subject and Body. Returns 1 on success.

inputs.name: overdue
inputs.description: List of Task instances that are past their due_date; passed to send_overdue_digest()

inputs.name: to
inputs.description: Recipient email address passed to send_overdue_digest()

outputs.name: sent
outputs.description: 0 if overdue list was empty and no email was sent; 1 after a successful SES send

description.high: The front door. Handles incoming HTTP requests from API Gateway and scheduled EventBridge events to manage tasks and send overdue notifications.
description.tech: Two AWS Lambda handler functions. lambda_handler routes API Gateway proxy events: GET /tasks calls TaskRepository.find() and returns a JSON list; POST /tasks parses the body, constructs a Task, calls TaskRepository.save(), and returns the saved task as JSON. notify_handler is triggered by EventBridge Scheduler: calls TaskRepository.find(status='pending'), filters tasks where due_date < datetime.utcnow(), and calls EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest().

blocks.name: lambda_handler

description.high: Receives HTTP requests from API Gateway and routes them to the right task operation — listing tasks or creating a new one.
description.tech: AWS Lambda handler function receiving an API Gateway proxy event dict and Lambda context. Routes on event['httpMethod'] and event['path']. GET /tasks: extracts optional 'status' from queryStringParameters, calls TaskRepository.find(status), serializes results to JSON (converting datetime to isoformat and handling Decimal for DynamoDB numeric types). POST /tasks: parses event['body'] as JSON, constructs Task(id=None, title=..., due_date=datetime.fromisoformat(...), priority=..., status=...), calls TaskRepository.save(task), returns saved task as JSON. All responses are API Gateway proxy response dicts with statusCode, headers (Content-Type: application/json), and a JSON-serialized body. Returns 405 for unsupported methods and 500 with error message on exceptions.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: API Gateway proxy event dict containing httpMethod, path, queryStringParameters, and body

outputs.name: response
outputs.description: API Gateway proxy response dict with statusCode (int), headers (dict), and body (JSON string)

blocks.name: notify_handler

description.high: Runs on a schedule to find all pending tasks that are past due and emails a digest to the configured recipient.
description.tech: AWS Lambda handler triggered by EventBridge Scheduler. Calls TaskRepository.find(status='pending') to retrieve all pending tasks, then filters to those where task.due_date < datetime.utcnow() to identify overdue tasks. Passes the overdue list to EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest(overdue, os.environ['NOTIFY_EMAIL']). Returns a summary dict with the count of overdue tasks and the SES send result. Raises KeyError if NOTIFY_EMAIL env var is not set.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: EventBridge Scheduler event dict (contents not used)

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Summary dict with 'overdue_count' (int) and 'sent' (0 or 1) keys

blocks.code:
import os
from datetime import datetime

def notify_handler(event: dict, context) -> dict:
    """EventBridge-triggered handler that sends overdue task digest via SES."""
    repo = TaskRepository()
    notifier = EmailNotifier()
    to_email = os.environ['NOTIFY_EMAIL']

    pending = repo.find(status='pending')
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    overdue = [t for t in pending if t.due_date.replace(tzinfo=None) < now]

    sent = notifier.send_overdue_digest(overdue, to_email)
    return {'overdue_count': len(overdue), 'sent': sent}

links.label: hydrates via from_row
links.description: Inside find(), TaskRepository calls Task.from_row(item) for every item dict returned by DynamoDB table.scan(), converting raw DynamoDB item dicts into Task dataclass instances.

dataStructure.name: DynamoDB item dict
dataStructure.format: Plain Python dict passed to Task.from_row()

fields.name: id
fields.description: UUID hex string assigned at save time

fields.name: due_date
fields.description: ISO-8601 datetime string; parsed by datetime.fromisoformat inside from_row()

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Priority level 1–3; stored as DynamoDB Number, cast to int in from_row()

links.label: provides list[Task]
links.description: TaskRepository.find(status='pending') produces the full list[Task] consumed by notify_handler, which then filters it to overdue tasks (due_date < utcnow()) and passes the resulting overdue list to EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest(). The notifier accesses task.title and task.due_date from each Task instance to build the digest body.

dataStructure.name: list[Task]
dataStructure.format: Python list of Task dataclass instances

fields.name: title
fields.description: Task title; rendered in the email body as '- {title} (due {due_date})'

fields.name: due_date
fields.description: Task due date; compared against utcnow() in notify_handler and formatted as %Y-%m-%d in the email body

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Priority level; present on the object but not used by EmailNotifier

fields.name: status
fields.description: Task status; filtered to 'pending' by find() before overdue check

links.label: send_overdue_digest
links.description: notify_handler filters the pending task list to overdue items and passes them to EmailNotifier.send_overdue_digest(overdue, to_email), which delivers the digest via Amazon SES.

fields.name: overdue
fields.description: Tasks with due_date < utcnow() and status 'pending'

fields.name: to
fields.description: Recipient email address from NOTIFY_EMAIL env var

links.label: find / save
links.description: lambda_handler instantiates TaskRepository and calls either find(status) for GET /tasks or save(task) for POST /tasks, delegating all DynamoDB persistence to the repository.

fields.name: task
fields.description: Task dataclass passed to save() on POST

fields.name: status
fields.description: Optional status string passed to find() on GET
